prompt: Set up a docker-compose test environment for this codebase.
expectations: Generates a valid docker-compose.yml (passes docker compose config)
Includes a postgres service using image postgres:15 or postgres:16
app service has DATABASE_URL env var using db service name (not localhost)
db service has a healthcheck defined
app service has depends_on: db with condition: service_healthy
docker compose up --wait succeeds with all services healthy
GET http://localhost:3000/health returns 200 with {status: ok}
common_failures: DATABASE_URL uses localhost instead of db service name
Missing depends_on or healthcheck causes race condition on startup
Port in DATABASE_URL uses wrong value (5433 vs 5432)

prompt: Create a local test environment with docker-compose for this project.
expectations: Generates a valid docker-compose.yml (passes docker compose config)
Includes api, worker, and redis services
worker service uses same image/build as api but different CMD (celery worker)
CELERY_BROKER_URL and CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND point to redis service name (not localhost)
worker depends_on redis with condition: service_healthy
redis has a healthcheck defined
docker compose up --wait succeeds with all services healthy
GET http://localhost:8000/health returns 200
POST http://localhost:8000/tasks returns 200 with task_id
common_failures: worker CMD not overridden — runs uvicorn instead of celery worker
CELERY_BROKER_URL uses localhost:6379 instead of redis:6379
redis missing healthcheck causes worker to start before broker ready

prompt: I need to run this app locally in docker for testing.
expectations: Generates a valid docker-compose.yml (passes docker compose config)
Includes a one-shot migrate service that runs: npx prisma migrate deploy
app service depends_on migrate with condition: service_completed_successfully
migrate service depends_on db with condition: service_healthy
DATABASE_URL in all services uses db service name (not localhost)
DATABASE_URL format is correct for Prisma: postgresql://user:pass@db:5432/dbname
docker compose up --wait succeeds with app healthy
GET http://localhost:3000/api/ping returns 200
common_failures: No migrate service — migrations not run, app crashes on first DB query
app depends_on db directly without waiting for migrations
DATABASE_URL missing ?sslmode=disable for local postgres (Prisma requires explicit SSL config)
Forgot to copy prisma/ directory into Docker build context

prompt: Set up a docker-compose environment for this multi-service Go repo.
expectations: Generates a valid docker-compose.yml (passes docker compose config)
svc-a and svc-b both defined with correct build contexts (./svc-a and ./svc-b)
svc-a has SVC_B_URL env var set to http://svc-b:8081 (service name, not localhost)
Services share a custom bridge network (or default compose network)
db has a healthcheck defined
docker compose up --wait succeeds with all services healthy
GET http://localhost:8080/health returns 200 from svc-a
GET http://localhost:8081/health returns 200 from svc-b
GET http://localhost:8080/ping-b returns 200 (svc-a successfully called svc-b)
common_failures: SVC_B_URL uses localhost:8081 — svc-a cannot reach svc-b this way inside Docker network
Both services mapped to same host port
build context wrong — Dockerfile not found because context points to repo root
